mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations. A sustained downward trend in FEDFUNDS over the preceding month signals monetary accommodation that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 1.3% suggests accommodative stance
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines do not exceed baseline returns by a statistically significant margin (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which increases corporate revenue expectations and equity valuations. A month-over-month increase in PAYEMS triggers positive 20-day forward returns as market reprices growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances where PAYEMS rises > 100k, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) directly reflect consumer spending power and near-term economic demand. Strong retail sales growth signals robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices earnings expectations upward.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant correlation with RSAFS growth (Spearman rank correlation < 0.10 over any 3-year window), or Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 2-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real economic output and capacity utilization. When INDPRO declines month-over-month, it signals weakening supply-side momentum and potential margin compression, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the market reprices profit expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances where INDPRO declines > 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 2%) signals monetary tightening that increases real discount rates and reduces equity valuations. The joint signal of high inflation and restrictive policy produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative when both conditions are met (mean return > 0% with t-test p > 0.10), or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression over 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer confidence and discretionary spending capacity. When both series show month-over-month growth, the combined signal indicates robust demand momentum that drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices cyclical earnings upward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases > 50k units AND RSAFS increases > 0.5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1,297 thousand units, RSAFS at 495,381 million dollars
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive when both conditions are met (mean return < 1% with t-test p > 0.10), or interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in regression analysis over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending power. When UNRATE increases > 0.3 percentage points over three months, it triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as the market reprices earnings downward due to weakening demand.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points over 3-month period
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly represents the broadest measure of economic demand. In late-cycle expansions where GDPC1 growth slows to < 2% annualized, the market reprices growth expectations downward, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as investors shift to defensive positioning.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate < 2.0% in latest quarter
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 17,163.894 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative when GDPC1 growth < 2% (mean return > 0.5% with t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
